titel: Swedish contribution to NATO's enhanced presence in Finland.
html: The proposal suggests that the Parliament should decide that the government may, until December 31, 2026, deploy Swedish armed forces, up to a maximum of 1,200 personnel, to contribute, in accordance with international law, to NATO's enhanced forward presence in Finland (eFP Finland). The Swedish force contribution in 2026 will be determined by NATO's operational planning.
tldr: Sweden plans to send up to 1,200 soldiers to Finland to assist NATO. This deployment is scheduled to take place by the end of 2026.
lattlast:
The Swedish government has proposed to the Swedish Parliament a plan to send Swedish soldiers to Finland. The purpose is to contribute to NATO's efforts to maintain a presence in Finland, a program known as FLF Finland. This is part of a broader collaboration in defense.

If the proposal is approved, the government will be able to deploy up to 1,200 personnel from the Swedish armed forces. This means that Swedish military personnel will be present in Finland to provide assistance. This operation is scheduled to continue until December 31, 2026.

The number of soldiers actually deployed in 2026 will depend on NATO's plans. NATO is a military organization where many countries cooperate for security. Therefore, NATO's leadership will determine the level of assistance needed in the various aspects of the plan.
